ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_age" INTEGER /* Age of the customer */,
  "customer_education" INTEGER /* Education level of the customer */,
  "months_as_customer" INTEGER /* Number of months the customer has been with the company */,
  "customer_gender_male" INTEGER /* Indicates if the customer is male */,
  "customer_gender_female" INTEGER /* Indicates if the customer is female */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "customer_email" TEXT /* Email address for communication */,
  "customer_phone" TEXT /* Phone number for contact */,
  "customer_address" TEXT /* Physical address for correspondence */,
  "customer_employment_status" TEXT /* Employment status of the customer */,
  "customer_income" INTEGER /* Annual income of the customer */,
  "customer_marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer */,
  "customer_children" INTEGER /* Number of children */,
  "customer_credit_score" INTEGER /* Credit score for risk assessment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer who holds the policy */,
  "policy_deductable" INTEGER /* Deductible amount for the policy */,
  "policy_annual_premium" INTEGER /* Annual premium for the policy */,
  "policy_liability" INTEGER /* Liability coverage for the policy */,
  "auto_year" INTEGER /* Year of the automobile */,
  "num_claims_past_year" INTEGER /* Number of claims in the past year */,
  "num_insurers_past_5_years" INTEGER /* Number of insurers in the past 5 years */,
  "policy_type" TEXT /* Type of policy (e.g., auto, home, life) */,
  "policy_start_date" TEXT /* Date when the policy was issued */,
  "policy_end_date" TEXT /* Date when the policy expires */,
  "policy_status" TEXT /* Current status of the policy (e.g., active, expired, canceled) */,
  "policy_coverage_limit" INTEGER /* Maximum coverage limit for the policy */,
  "policy_payment_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of premium payments (e.g., monthly, annually) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policies_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policies_num_insurers_past_5_years FOREIGN KEY ("num_insurers_past_5_years") REFERENCES insurers ("insurer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policy_states" (
  "policy_state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy state record */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the policy */,
  "state_code" TEXT /* State code where the policy is active */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state where the policy is active */,
  "state_population" INTEGER /* Population of the state for demographic analysis */,
  "state_climate" TEXT /* Climate type of the state for risk assessment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_state_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policy_states_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "claims" (
  "claim_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each claim */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the policy associated with the claim */,
  "claim_date" TEXT /* Date when the claim was filed */,
  "claim_amount" INTEGER /* Amount claimed */,
  "claim_status" TEXT /* Status of the claim (e.g., pending, approved, denied) */,
  "claim_description" TEXT /* Description of the claim */,
  "claim_resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the claim was resolved */,
  "claim_payout_amount" INTEGER /* Amount paid out for the claim */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("claim_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_claims_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "insurers" (
  "insurer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each insurer */,
  "insurer_name" TEXT /* Name of the insurer */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the insurer */,
  "insurer_address" TEXT /* Physical address of the insurer */,
  "insurer_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the insurer */,
  "insurer_website" TEXT /* Website of the insurer */,
  "insurer_rating" TEXT /* Rating of the insurer (e.g., A, B, C) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("insurer_id")
)
